Class MainController

java.lang.Object
com.library.controller.MainController
public class MainController extends Object
JavaFX controller for MainView.fxml.

Wires together the four main entity tabs (Books, Authors, Readers, Loans), each providing:

  • CRUD — Add / Edit / Delete via modal dialogs
  • Live search — results update on each keystroke or filter change (no explicit "Search" button)
  • Filters — genre/country/status ComboBoxes and year/date range fields

All database access is delegated to the DAO layer; no SQL appears here. All dialogs perform client-side validation before allowing the OK action.

Architecture: View (FXML) → MainController → DAO → PostgreSQL

See Also:
  • BookDao
  • AuthorDao
  • ReaderDao
  • LoanDao

    Reads all active filter values and delegates to AuthorDao.search(String, String, Integer, Integer).

    applyBooksFilter()
    Reads all active filter values and delegates to BookDao.search(String, String, Integer, Integer).
    private void
    applyLoansFilter()
    Reads all active filter values and delegates to LoanDao.search(String, String, LocalDate, LocalDate).
    private void
    applyReadersFilter()
    Reads all active filter values and delegates to ReaderDao.search(String, LocalDate, LocalDate).

    authorComboBox()
    Creates an Author ComboBox populated with all authors from the database.

    coalesce(String s)
    Trims and returns the string, or null if it is blank.

    confirm(String msg)
    Shows a YES/NO confirmation dialog.

    dialog(String title)
    Creates a generic Dialog with OK and Cancel buttons and no header text.

    Creates a GridPane with standard padding and gap settings used by all Add/Edit dialogs.

    Populates the country ComboBox from the database and attaches change listeners so the table reacts immediately to any filter change.

    Attaches change listeners to reader filter controls so the table reacts immediately to any change.

    Creates a right-aligned Label with a fixed minimum width, used as a form row label in dialog grids.

    Refreshes the country ComboBox items after a new author has been added.

    Converts an int to a display string, returning an empty string for 0 (the "not set" sentinel used in the models).

    strConv(Function<T,String> fn)
    Creates a lightweight StringConverter from a single toString lambda (the fromString direction is unused).
    private static int
    toInt(String s)
    Parses a string to int, returning 0 on blank or invalid input.
    private static boolean
    validEmail(String s)
    Validates an optional email address using a basic structural pattern.
    private static boolean
    validIsbn(String s)
    Validates an optional ISBN value.
    private static boolean
    validPhone(String s)
    Validates an optional phone number.
    private static boolean
    validPositiveInt(String s)
    Validates that a string represents a positive integer (for the Copies field).
    private static boolean
    validYear(String s)
    Validates an optional year field.
    private void
    warn(String msg)
    Shows a WARNING alert for "no row selected" situations.

  • Constructor Details

    • MainController

      public MainController()

  • Method Details

    • initialize

      public void initialize()
      Called automatically by FXMLLoader after all @FXML fields have been injected.

      Sets up cell-value factories, populates filter ComboBoxes from the database, attaches change listeners, and performs an initial data load.

    • setupBooksTable

      private void setupBooksTable()
      Binds Book properties to the corresponding table columns.

    • initBookFilters

      private void initBookFilters()
      Populates the genre ComboBox from the database and attaches change listeners so the table reacts immediately to any filter change.

    • applyBooksFilter

      private void applyBooksFilter()
      Reads all active filter values and delegates to BookDao.search(String, String, Integer, Integer). Updates booksData and the status bar with the result count.

    • onClearBooksFilter

      private void onClearBooksFilter()
      Resets all book filter controls; change listeners trigger applyBooksFilter() automatically.

    • onAddBook

      private void onAddBook()
      Opens the Add Book dialog; inserts on OK.

    • onEditBook

      private void onEditBook()
      Opens the Edit Book dialog pre-filled with the selected row; updates on OK.

    • onDeleteBook

      private void onDeleteBook()
      Confirms and deletes the selected book.

      If the book is referenced by loans, the DAO throws a RuntimeException with a descriptive message that is shown to the user as an error dialog.

    • refreshBookFilters

      private void refreshBookFilters()
      Refreshes the genre ComboBox items after a new book has been added (the new book's genre may not yet be in the list).

    • showBookDialog

      private Optional<Book> showBookDialog(Book existing)
      Builds and shows the Book dialog in Add or Edit mode.

      Validation rules enforced before OK is accepted:

      • Title — required (non-blank)
      • Author — required (ComboBox selection)
      • ISBN — if provided, must be 10 or 13 digits (hyphens allowed)
      • Pub. year — if provided, must be 1–4 digits
      • Copies — must be a positive integer
      • Year range — yearFrom ≤ yearTo when both are provided
      Parameters:
      existing - null for Add mode; a populated Book for Edit mode
      Returns:
      an Optional containing the user-filled book, or empty on Cancel

    • setupAuthorsTable

      private void setupAuthorsTable()
      Binds Author properties to the corresponding table columns.

    • initAuthorFilters

      private void initAuthorFilters()
      Populates the country ComboBox from the database and attaches change listeners so the table reacts immediately to any filter change.

    • applyAuthorsFilter

      private void applyAuthorsFilter()
      Reads all active filter values and delegates to AuthorDao.search(String, String, Integer, Integer). Updates authorsData and the status bar with the result count.

    • onClearAuthorsFilter

      private void onClearAuthorsFilter()
      Resets all author filter controls; change listeners trigger applyAuthorsFilter() automatically.

    • onAddAuthor

      private void onAddAuthor()
      Opens the Add Author dialog; inserts on OK.

    • onEditAuthor

      private void onEditAuthor()
      Opens the Edit Author dialog pre-filled with the selected row; updates on OK.

    • onDeleteAuthor

      private void onDeleteAuthor()
      Confirms and deletes the selected author.

      If the author still has books in the library, the DAO throws a RuntimeException which is displayed as an error dialog.

    • refreshAuthorFilters

      private void refreshAuthorFilters()
      Refreshes the country ComboBox items after a new author has been added.

    • showAuthorDialog

      private Optional<Author> showAuthorDialog(Author existing)
      Builds and shows the Author dialog in Add or Edit mode.

      Validation rules enforced before OK is accepted:

      • Full name — required (non-blank)
      • Birth year — if provided, must be 1–4 digits
      • Year range — yearFrom ≤ yearTo when both are provided in filters
      Parameters:
      existing - null for Add mode; a populated Author for Edit mode
      Returns:
      an Optional containing the user-filled author, or empty on Cancel

    • setupReadersTable

      private void setupReadersTable()
      Binds Reader properties to the corresponding table columns.

    • initReaderFilters

      private void initReaderFilters()
      Attaches change listeners to reader filter controls so the table reacts immediately to any change.

    • applyReadersFilter

      private void applyReadersFilter()
      Reads all active filter values and delegates to ReaderDao.search(String, LocalDate, LocalDate). Updates readersData and the status bar with the result count.

    • onClearReadersFilter

      private void onClearReadersFilter()
      Resets all reader filter controls; change listeners trigger applyReadersFilter() automatically.

    • onAddReader

      private void onAddReader()
      Opens the Add Reader dialog; inserts on OK.

    • onEditReader

      private void onEditReader()
      Opens the Edit Reader dialog pre-filled with the selected row; updates on OK.

    • onDeleteReader

      private void onDeleteReader()
      Confirms and deletes the selected reader.

      If the reader still has loan records, the DAO throws a RuntimeException which is displayed as an error dialog.

    • showReaderDialog

      private Optional<Reader> showReaderDialog(Reader existing)
      Builds and shows the Reader dialog in Add or Edit mode.

      Validation rules enforced before OK is accepted:

      • Full name — required (non-blank)
      • Email — if provided, must match a basic name@domain.tld pattern
      • Phone — if provided, must contain 7–15 digits (spaces, hyphens, parentheses and a leading + are allowed)
      • Registration date — required
      Parameters:
      existing - null for Add mode; a populated Reader for Edit mode
      Returns:
      an Optional containing the user-filled reader, or empty on Cancel

    • setupLoansTable

      private void setupLoansTable()
      Binds Loan properties to the corresponding table columns. The status column uses a custom cell factory to colour-code the text: green for active, red for overdue, grey for returned.

    • initLoanFilters

      private void initLoanFilters()
      Populates the status ComboBox and attaches change listeners so the table reacts immediately to any filter change.

    • applyLoansFilter

      private void applyLoansFilter()
      Reads all active filter values and delegates to LoanDao.search(String, String, LocalDate, LocalDate). Updates loansData and the status bar with the result count.

    • onClearLoansFilter

      private void onClearLoansFilter()
      Resets all loan filter controls; change listeners trigger applyLoansFilter() automatically.

    • onAddLoan

      private void onAddLoan()
      Opens the Add Loan dialog; inserts on OK.

    • onEditLoan

      private void onEditLoan()
      Opens the Edit Loan dialog pre-filled with the selected row; updates on OK.

    • onDeleteLoan

      private void onDeleteLoan()
      Confirms and deletes the selected loan.

    • showLoanDialog

      private Optional<Loan> showLoanDialog(Loan existing)
      Builds and shows the Loan dialog in Add or Edit mode.

      Validation rules enforced before OK is accepted:

      • Book — required (ComboBox selection)
      • Reader — required (ComboBox selection)
      • Due date — required and must be on or after the loan date
      Parameters:
      existing - null for Add mode; a populated Loan for Edit mode
      Returns:
      an Optional containing the user-filled loan, or empty on Cancel

    • dialog

      private <T> javafx.scene.control.Dialog<T> dialog(String title)
      Creates a generic Dialog with OK and Cancel buttons and no header text.
      Type Parameters:
      T - result type of the dialog
      Parameters:
      title - dialog window title
      Returns:
      a configured but not yet shown dialog

    • grid

      private javafx.scene.layout.GridPane grid()
      Creates a GridPane with standard padding and gap settings used by all Add/Edit dialogs.
      Returns:
      a configured GridPane

    • okFilter

      private void okFilter(javafx.scene.control.Dialog<?> dlg, Supplier<String> validator)
      Attaches a validation filter to the OK button of a dialog.

      When the user clicks OK, the validator is called. If it returns a non-empty string, the action is consumed (dialog stays open) and an error alert is shown listing all validation failures.

      Parameters:
      dlg - the dialog whose OK button to guard
      validator - a supplier that returns an empty string on success, or a newline-separated list of error messages on failure

    • authorComboBox

      private javafx.scene.control.ComboBox<Author> authorComboBox()
      Creates an Author ComboBox populated with all authors from the database.
      Returns:
      a configured, ready-to-use ComboBox

    • strConv

      private <T> javafx.util.StringConverter<T> strConv(Function<T,String> fn)
      Creates a lightweight StringConverter from a single toString lambda (the fromString direction is unused).
      Type Parameters:
      T - item type
      Parameters:
      fn - function mapping an item to its display string
      Returns:
      a StringConverter backed by fn

    • setStatus

      private void setStatus(String msg)
      Updates the status bar label with the given message.
      Parameters:
      msg - the message to display

    • warn

      private void warn(String msg)
      Shows a WARNING alert for "no row selected" situations.
      Parameters:
      msg - the message to display

    • confirm

      private boolean confirm(String msg)
      Shows a YES/NO confirmation dialog.
      Parameters:
      msg - the confirmation question
      Returns:
      true if the user clicked YES

    • showAlert

      private void showAlert(javafx.scene.control.Alert.AlertType type, String title, String msg)
      Shows a generic alert dialog.
      Parameters:
      type - alert type (ERROR, WARNING, INFORMATION, etc.)
      title - window title
      msg - message body

    • field

      private static javafx.scene.control.TextField field(String value, String prompt)
      Creates a styled TextField with a preset value and prompt text.
      Parameters:
      value - initial value (may be blank)
      prompt - placeholder text shown when the field is empty
      Returns:
      a configured TextField with prefWidth = 220

    • label

      private static javafx.scene.control.Label label(String text)
      Creates a right-aligned Label with a fixed minimum width, used as a form row label in dialog grids.
      Parameters:
      text - label text
      Returns:
      a configured Label

    • str

      private static String str(int v)
      Converts an int to a display string, returning an empty string for 0 (the "not set" sentinel used in the models).
      Parameters:
      v - the integer value
      Returns:
      "" if v == 0, otherwise String.valueOf(v)

    • toInt

      private static int toInt(String s)
      Parses a string to int, returning 0 on blank or invalid input.
      Parameters:
      s - the string to parse
      Returns:
      the parsed integer, or 0

    • parseIntOrNull

      private static Integer parseIntOrNull(String s)
      Parses a string to an Integer, returning null on blank or invalid input (used for optional filter range bounds).
      Parameters:
      s - the string to parse
      Returns:
      the parsed integer, or null

    • coalesce

      private static String coalesce(String s)
      Trims and returns the string, or null if it is blank. Used to map empty dialog fields to SQL NULL.
      Parameters:
      s - the string value
      Returns:
      trimmed string, or null

    • validYear

      private static boolean validYear(String s)
      Validates an optional year field.
      Parameters:
      s - the field value; blank is treated as "not provided" (valid).
      Returns:
      true if blank, or contains a valid integer between -4000 and the current year.

    • validPositiveInt

      private static boolean validPositiveInt(String s)
      Validates that a string represents a positive integer (for the Copies field).
      Parameters:
      s - the field value; blank is treated as "not provided" (valid)
      Returns:
      true if blank or a string of one or more digits

    • validEmail

      private static boolean validEmail(String s)
      Validates an optional email address using a basic structural pattern.

      Pattern: localPart@domain.tld where each part contains word characters, dots, plus signs, or hyphens. Full RFC 5321 compliance is intentionally not enforced here.

      Parameters:
      s - the email string to validate (must not be blank before calling)
      Returns:
      true if the string loosely matches an email format

    • validPhone

      private static boolean validPhone(String s)
      Validates an optional phone number.

      Accepts an optional leading +, followed by 7–15 digits; spaces, hyphens, and parentheses are stripped before counting digits.

      Parameters:
      s - the phone string to validate (must not be blank before calling)
      Returns:
      true if the string contains 7–15 digit characters

    • validIsbn

      private static boolean validIsbn(String s)
      Validates an optional ISBN value.

      Accepts ISBN-10 (10 digits) and ISBN-13 (13 digits); hyphens used as separators are stripped before counting. The check digit is not mathematically verified.

      Parameters:
      s - the ISBN string to validate (must not be blank before calling)
      Returns:
      true if the digit-only form has exactly 10 or 13 characters
